last_report:
### CANCELLED — Boston Celtics ML (Draft Pick nba_draft_20260329_001)
- Reason: **Anchor Jaylen Brown OUT confirmed at commit time.** NBA official injury report (nba_official source) lists Brown as "Out — Tendinitis." At Scout time, Brown was Questionable. Skills rule is unambiguous: "Anchor player confirmed OUT → cancel immediately." Brown is listed as an anchor player and is an All-Star level player whose absence fundamentally changes the Celtics' offensive ceiling. Additionally, Derrick White remains Questionable (Right Knee Contusion). Tatum does appear to be active (not on injury report), which is a positive, but Brown's confirmed OUT status alone triggers immediate cancellation. Even if we attempted to re-evaluate: confidence would drop from 62 to ~47 (Brown OUT -10, White Q -5), and EV at those odds would be subthreshold.
- Odds at cancel: 2.00 Charlotte ML / 1.82 Boston ML (Boston was 1.89 at Scout — moved slightly unfavorably: -0.07 delta, minor)

---

### CONFIRMED (LATE PICK) — Orlando Magic ML
- Match: Toronto Raptors vs Orlando Magic | Tip: 2026-03-29T22:00 UTC
- Pick: Orlando Magic ML | Scout odds: N/A (new late pick) | Commit odds: 2.04 | Scout line was 2.14 (moved -0.10 since Scout rejection evaluation)
- ML: H:1.80 (Toronto) / A:2.04 (Orlando) | ATS: Toronto -1.5 (1.89/1.93) | O/U: N/A
- Stake: €65.30 | Pot: €133.21 | Conf: 60/100 | EV: +22.4%
- Decision: Material late-breaking change. Franz Wagner was listed OUT at Scout time, triggering Orlando's rejection. Current NBA official injury report does **not** list Wagner — he is available to play. This is a significant upgrade. Simultaneously, Toronto carries three questionable starters: Ingram (Inflammation), Barrett (Stiffness), Jackson-Davis (Knee Tendinitis). The teams are essentially evenly matched by record (41-32 Toronto vs 39-34 Orlando) and NetRtg (+1.5 vs +0.8). With Wagner active and Toronto potentially missing key pieces, Orlando at 2.04 away represents genuine value. Line moved from 2.14 → 2.04, suggesting books are partially pricing Wagner in, but the move is only -0.10 (within allowable drift). EV = +22.4% well above 5% floor. All gates satisfied.

---

**Slate Summary:** A single-bet commit day driven primarily by late injury news. The Celtics ML draft pick collapsed at commit time when Jaylen Brown was confirmed OUT — a case where the Scout's own warning ("re-evaluate at Commit if Tatum AND Brown OUT") proved prescient. The sole confirmed bet leverages Franz Wagner's clearance from the injury report combined with Toronto's multi-player questionable situation, creating genuine underdog value in what is fundamentally an even matchup. Total exposure: €65.30 (15.0% of bankroll), well within limits. Bankroll remaining after commitment: €369.91.

pending_bets.reasoning: LATE PICK — Franz Wagner status change since 14:00 Scout. Scout rejected Orlando ML because Wagner was listed OUT (key franchise-adjacent player). Current NBA official injury report does NOT list Wagner — he appears cleared and active. This is a material upgrade for Orlando. Meanwhile Toronto has THREE key players questionable: Brandon Ingram (Q-Inflammation), RJ Barrett (Q-Stiffness), Trayce Jackson-Davis (Q-Knee Tendinitis). Toronto (41-32, NetRtg +1.5) vs Orlando (39-34, NetRtg +0.8) — nearly identical records and ratings. With Toronto's depth at risk and Wagner likely active, Orlando at 2.04 offers genuine value. Line moved from 2.14 → 2.04 (-0.10) which is consistent with books partially adjusting for Wagner's availability, but hasn't fully priced in Toronto's multi-player questionable situation. Confidence build: Base 50 (even teams), Toronto home advantage offset (-5 for Orlando), Toronto 3 key players Q (+10), Wagner active upgrade (+10), Orlando's Isaac/Black OUT holes (-5) = 60. EV = (0.60 × 2.04) - 1 = +22.4%. Odds 2.04 in valid ML range (1.65-2.50). Injury source: nba_official (no cap restrictions). Time gate: 22:00 UTC tip, now 19:52 = 128 min > 20 min. Stake: Medium tier (55-69 confidence) = 15% × €435.31 = €65.30.

At commit time, odds improved to 2.38 (+0.13 in our favour), consistent with news that Luka Doncic (Questionable, Right Hip) and LeBron James (Questionable, Left Foot Arthritis) are both uncertain for Lakers â€” two franchise-level players potentially unavailable. Austin Reaves also Questionable. Heat are missing Jaquez Jr. (confirmed OUT, was Questionable at Scout), Andrew Wiggins (OUT, new), and Terry Rozier (OUT, new), but their core rotation (Butler, Adebayo) remains intact. Net injury impact heavily favours Heat: Lakers potentially missing two franchise players outweighs Heat's role-player absences. Books have moved from 2.25 to 2.38, partially pricing in LAL risks, but 2.38 still offers strong value. EV at commit odds: (0.53 Ã— 2.38) - 1 = 0.2614 = 26.1%, well above 0.05 threshold. Original stake unchanged per rules (do not increase even on improved odds). Total exposure: 10% of bankroll.

Boston missing Nikola Vucevic (OUT, fracture) — already priced in. OKC's only absentees are G League assignments (Barnhizer, Sandfort, Topic, Sorber). NetRtg gap of +3.6 pts in OKC's favour, with home court reducing effective gap to ~0.6–1.6 pts, but OKC's dominance (11.2 NetRtg, 11-game win streak) justifies road favourite pricing. Star rest risk minimal — road game at top-5 team (playoff seeding implications). Odds improved +0.08 at commit; confirming at original stake per line movement rules. EV = (0.68 × 1.79) - 1 = 0.217 (21.7%). Odds 1.79 within 1.70–2.50 ML target range.

settled_bets.match: Oklahoma City Thunder vs Chicago Bulls
settled_bets.reasoning: OKC #1 seed (57-16, L10: 9-1) with elite NetRtg +10.9 vs Chicago's -4.4 — gap of 15.3 pts, well above 6pt spread threshold. SGA and Dort both confirmed active per NBA official injury report. Chet Holmgren listed OUT (hip) — not an anchor player; adjusted confidence from 72 to 67 (non-anchor significant player out). Chicago equally decimated: Collins OUT, Essengue OUT, Jaden Ivey OUT (new since Scout), Nick Richards Questionable — frontcourt gutted. OKC DefRtg 107.7 vs Chicago OffRtg 113.5; Chicago DefRtg 118.0 (porous). Odds improved marginally from 1.92 to 1.93 (in our favor). EV at confidence 67, odds 1.93: (0.67 × 1.93) - 1 = 0.293. Well above 0.05 threshold. Spread within valid range (1.75–2.30 floor at commit). Injury source: nba_official — no data quality restrictions apply. Stake: 15% of bankroll (medium confidence tier) = €458.24 × 0.15 = €68.74.

Detroit (53-20, L10: 8-2, NetRtg +7.8) is the materially superior team by record and efficiency vs Minnesota (45-28, L10: 5-5, NetRtg +3.6). Anthony Edwards (Minnesota franchise player) is confirmed OUT with patellofemoral pain, joined by Jaden McDaniels and Ayo Dosunmu. Cade Cunningham is also OUT for Detroit (pneumothorax), applying -10 confidence and -20% stake reduction per franchise_player_rules. Despite both teams missing key players, Detroit's underlying quality advantage (8-game record gap, +4.2 NetRtg differential, far superior L10 form) combined with Minnesota's triple-injury blow justifies confidence in Detroit at near-even odds. Detroit ML at 1.98 clears the odds floor (1.65) comfortably. EV = (0.65 × 1.98) - 1 = 0.287. Stake reduced 20% for Cunningham OUT.

picks.reasoning: Boston (49-24, L10: 7-3, NetRtg +7.7) vs Charlotte (39-35, L10: 7-3, NetRtg +4.8). NetRtg gap: +2.9 points in Boston's favor — modest but real. Charlotte's NetRtg +4.8 is genuine, and they have play-in motivation. However, Boston's elite defensive rating (112.5 DefRtg vs Charlotte's 114.6) gives a meaningful edge. CRITICAL INJURY NOTE: Jaylen Brown (Questionable-Tendinitis), Jayson Tatum (Questionable-Management), Derrick White (Questionable-Knee) — THREE key Celtics are questionable. This is a severe concern. Applying franchise-player-adjacent rule: Tatum = franchise player level → confidence -10. Brown = All-Star → confidence -5 additional. Derrick White → -3. Base confidence starts at ~75 (Boston road favourite vs play-in team, NetRtg advantage, elite roster depth). Adjustments: Tatum Questionable -10, Brown Questionable -5, White Questionable -3 = adjusted confidence 57. No B2B for either team. Charlotte has play-in motivation (+5 partial offset) → final confidence 62. Odds 1.89 are in ML target range (1.70-2.50). EV = (0.62 × 1.89) - 1 = 1.1718 - 1 = 0.1718. EV = +17.2% — strong edge above 5% floor. Stake: Medium tier (55-69 confidence) = 15% of €435.31 = €65.30. NOTE: If Tatum AND Brown are both confirmed OUT at tip-off, this pick should be re-evaluated at Commit phase — edge may deteriorate significantly.

scout_report_data.report:
**Charlotte Hornets vs Boston Celtics — DRAFTED: Boston Celtics ML @ 1.89 (Conf 62, EV +17.2%)**
Boston's elite NetRtg +7.7 and DefRtg 112.5 give a clear edge over Charlotte despite the Hornets' genuine +4.8 NetRtg and play-in motivation. The primary risk is the triple-Questionable injury cluster (Tatum/Brown/White) — Commit phase MUST verify starting lineups before staking. If Tatum and Brown both play, confidence upgrades to ~77 and this becomes a High-tier pick. If both sit, this pick should be withdrawn.

**Oklahoma City Thunder vs New York Knicks — DRAFTED: OKC -8.5 @ 1.92 (Conf 60, EV +15.2%)**
OKC's league-best NetRtg +10.9 and home-court advantage support a spread cover, with NYK missing guard depth (Shamet OUT, McBride Q). The critical caveat: OKC is locked into the #1 seed with 58-16 record, making SGA rest a real possibility. Commit phase MUST verify OKC starters — if SGA sits, do NOT commit.

**Milwaukee Bucks vs LA Clippers** — REJECTED: Clippers ML 1.09 outside all target ranges; Clippers stats unavailable for spread eval; Giannis confirmed OUT justifies line but no bettable market.
**Indiana Pacers vs Miami Heat** — REJECTED: Heat ML 1.26 outside range; Pacers confirmed tanking (16-58) with Haliburton OUT, Siakam Q — no bet on Pacers; no valid spread edge.
**Brooklyn Nets vs Sacramento Kings** — NO EDGE: Both confirmed tanking teams; neither at ≥2.20 required for tanking-team bets; coin-flip matchup.
**Toronto Raptors vs Orlando Magic** — NO EDGE: NetRtg gap too small (+0.7); Franz Wagner OUT depletes Orlando; Ingram Q for Toronto pulls confidence below ML floor.
**Portland Trail Blazers vs Washington Wizards** — REJECTED: Portland ML 1.08 not bettable; spread -16 EV only +3.95% (below 5% floor); Portland itself heavily depleted.
**New Orleans Pelicans vs Houston Rockets** — NO EDGE: Houston ML 1.43 outside range; Pelicans at 2.88 requires Murray/Murphy to play AND Rockets to underperform — cannot build thesis on opponent absence.
**Denver Nuggets vs Golden State Warriors** — REJECTED: Denver ML 1.15 outside range; Stephen Curry OUT = franchise player rule blocks Warriors ML; Warriors roster too depleted to bet.

**Slate quality: Moderate.** Two genuine edges identified but both are heavily injury-dependent and require Commit-phase verification. The slate is dominated by mismatches (Wizards/Pacers/Nets/Kings games) and extreme odds that fall outside target ranges. Pace data universally unavailable (0.0 in feed) — all O/U signals suppressed this session.

Atlanta -14.5 spread at 1.90 has genuine value (Sacramento devastated: LaVine/Sabonis/Murray/Hunter/Eubanks all OUT, NetRtg gap 12.1, Atlanta L10: 8-2). Confidence 65, EV 0.235. However, the max 1 new pick per Commit phase limit is already used by the Detroit ML pick, which has superior EV (0.287 vs 0.235) and cleaner thesis. Both ML markets outside valid range.

Memphis ML at 1.56 below 1.65 floor. Chicago ML at 2.48 within ceiling but confidence on Chicago cannot reach 60 threshold required for late scout — both teams are bottom-tier (Chicago 29-44, 3-7 L10; Memphis 24-49, 1-9 L10), Chicago missing Collins/Essengue/Ivey/Richards, near coin-flip matchup. Confidence ~50 — speculative tier fails the ≥60 gate for new Commit picks.

Phoenix ML at 1.08 and Utah ML at 8.5 both far outside valid ML range. Spread now Phoenix -17.0 (moved from -16.5 against Utah), Phoenix still carrying 4 key players OUT (Brooks, Coffey, Highsmith, Williams). Same rejection basis as Scout — Phoenix injury load too high to trust covering -17.0.

commit_report_data.report:
### CANCELLED — Boston Celtics ML (Draft Pick nba_draft_20260329_001)
- Reason: **Anchor Jaylen Brown OUT confirmed at commit time.** NBA official injury report (nba_official source) lists Brown as "Out — Tendinitis." At Scout time, Brown was Questionable. Skills rule is unambiguous: "Anchor player confirmed OUT → cancel immediately." Brown is listed as an anchor player and is an All-Star level player whose absence fundamentally changes the Celtics' offensive ceiling. Additionally, Derrick White remains Questionable (Right Knee Contusion). Tatum does appear to be active (not on injury report), which is a positive, but Brown's confirmed OUT status alone triggers immediate cancellation. Even if we attempted to re-evaluate: confidence would drop from 62 to ~47 (Brown OUT -10, White Q -5), and EV at those odds would be subthreshold.
- Odds at cancel: 2.00 Charlotte ML / 1.82 Boston ML (Boston was 1.89 at Scout — moved slightly unfavorably: -0.07 delta, minor)

---

### CONFIRMED (LATE PICK) — Orlando Magic ML
- Match: Toronto Raptors vs Orlando Magic | Tip: 2026-03-29T22:00 UTC
- Pick: Orlando Magic ML | Scout odds: N/A (new late pick) | Commit odds: 2.04 | Scout line was 2.14 (moved -0.10 since Scout rejection evaluation)
- ML: H:1.80 (Toronto) / A:2.04 (Orlando) | ATS: Toronto -1.5 (1.89/1.93) | O/U: N/A
- Stake: €65.30 | Pot: €133.21 | Conf: 60/100 | EV: +22.4%
- Decision: Material late-breaking change. Franz Wagner was listed OUT at Scout time, triggering Orlando's rejection. Current NBA official injury report does **not** list Wagner — he is available to play. This is a significant upgrade. Simultaneously, Toronto carries three questionable starters: Ingram (Inflammation), Barrett (Stiffness), Jackson-Davis (Knee Tendinitis). The teams are essentially evenly matched by record (41-32 Toronto vs 39-34 Orlando) and NetRtg (+1.5 vs +0.8). With Wagner active and Toronto potentially missing key pieces, Orlando at 2.04 away represents genuine value. Line moved from 2.14 → 2.04, suggesting books are partially pricing Wagner in, but the move is only -0.10 (within allowable drift). EV = +22.4% well above 5% floor. All gates satisfied.

---

**Slate Summary:** A single-bet commit day driven primarily by late injury news. The Celtics ML draft pick collapsed at commit time when Jaylen Brown was confirmed OUT — a case where the Scout's own warning ("re-evaluate at Commit if Tatum AND Brown OUT") proved prescient. The sole confirmed bet leverages Franz Wagner's clearance from the injury report combined with Toronto's multi-player questionable situation, creating genuine underdog value in what is fundamentally an even matchup. Total exposure: €65.30 (15.0% of bankroll), well within limits. Bankroll remaining after commitment: €369.91.
